Interpretation 3. In this Order, unless the context otherwise requires-
" assistant bicycle assembler" means an employee who--
(i) assembles perambulators, go-carts, scooters, express
wagons, cans or any wheeled vehicle or toys propelled
by hand or by foot, where the assembling does not
involve the adjustment of precision bearings, chains
or built-in wheels or the setting and tightening of
spokes; or
(ii) under general supervision, assembles bicycles from
semi-knocked down units or any components parts,
where the assembling does not involve truing of
bicycle wheels, the final adjustment of precision
bearing chains or built in wheels or the setting and
tightening ofs pokes;
"assistant clerk" means an employee engaged in clerical work
who has three years experience and whose education
qualification

"employee" means a person employed in a shop or in connection
with the busiriess ofa shop and who has entered into or works
under a contract of service whether oral or written and whether
express or implied but excludes an apprentice;
"employer'.'means a person who engages an employee to work
under a contract of service, whether oral or written and
whether express ot imp! ied,.in a shop, or. in connection with
the business of a shop;

sections.text:

in accordance with the following tables:

В·

worker, not elsewhere specified;

or goods lift;

ordinary driving licence;

General 2. (!) The wages or any benefit enjoyed by an employee shall
Provisions not be reduced oraltered adversely as atesult of the application of this
Order. В· В· В· В·В·В· В·
( 2) Where, prior to the introduction of this Order, an employee
was in receipt ofa wage in excess of the.-minimilmrate of pay on any
grade, the employee shall retain such wage rateВ·and in addition such
wage shall be increased by thirty-three and one-third per cent which
will allow identical credit, for length of service or experience or bqth,
which the employee earned in thate mployees' existing grade or category
at the date of the coming into force ofth_is Order. В·
(3) В·A n employeпїЅ shall not suffer any reduction in pay upon transfer
from a lo-:ver grade to a higher grade. В·
В·
(4) A part-time employeпїЅ or caпїЅual employee shall be paid wages
appropriate _to that employee's grade in proportion that the hours
worked by the employee per month bears to one hundred and ninetyВ­
five hours.

!8th January, 2 00 2 Statutory Instruments

sections.heading: Hours of (!) A person shall not employ an employee, other than a Work manager-
sections.text:
(a) for more than forty-five hours in a week excluding meal hours;
(b) for more than eight and one-half hours in a day, excluding
meal hours, except in the case ofbutcheries, bakeries, and dairies who shall compete within ten and one-halfhours of
commencement of such employees' work;
(c) for more than five hours without an interval of at least one
hour for a meal;
(d) to work after 140 0h ours for more than five days in a week;
(e) under sixteen years but not below fifteen years of age for
m<lte than seven hours in a day confined within a period of
ten hours; or
(j) on Sunday or public holiday.
( 2) For the purpose ofs tock-taking or the sale of perishable foodВ­
staffo r other emergency work which cannot be canied out during normal
working hours, an employee may be employed on a Sunday or public
holiday'or beyond the hours prescribed in sub-paragraph (I).
(3) Weekly hours for a watchman shall be sixty, spread over six
as
В·
days, and any hour worked in excess shall be paid provided in
paragraph 4 of this Schedule.

sections.heading: (!)Any employee who works in'iixпїЅпїЅi1 offortyCfivehours in a Overtime
sections.text:
week shall be paid at one and halftimes the employee's hourly rate of
. . .
pay.
( 2) An employee shall be paid for work done on sunday or.public
holidays by the employer at an hourly.rate of not less than double the
employee's actual hourly rate of pay.
(3) To calculate the hourly rate of pay in a mounth, the actual amount
received by the employee in basic wages for that months shall be divided
by one hundred and ninety-five hours.

sections.heading: (!)An employer shall grant leave of absence on full pay to an Pai9 leave
sections.text:
employee, other than a part-time employee, at the rate oft wo days per
montgh to an employee 'Yho has completed six months continous service
subject to and in accordance with, the following conditions-'-
(a) in computing the period of!eave, Sundays and l'ublic holidays
shall be excluded;
(b) part-time employees shall be given leave ofabsence on full
pay as set out in sub-paragraph (I) based on the proportion
that their number ofhoiIrs worked per month bears to one .
hundred and ninety-five hours; and
(c) lhe employer shall give reason.able consideration to the exigencies and interest of the business of the employer in
agreeing to the date when an employee may take leave.

Sick leave 6. (I) An employee who, according to the judgement ofa registered
medical practitionerormedical institution designated by the employer,
is tmable to execute their work by reason ofs ickness or accident shall
on producing a medical certificate be granted sick leave as follows:
(a) during probationary period,

sections.heading: An employee may be declared redundant, subject to negotiation Redundancy
sections.text:
with the trade union where appropriate but ifthere is no trade union
representing the employee in the shop, the employer shall given such
redundancy benefit of not less than two months pay for each completed
year_ of service.
1 0. (I) The nonnal retirement age shall be fifty-five years. Retirement
(2 ) An employee who has served for a minimum period often age
years shall be entitled retirement benefits of two and one half months
for each completed year of service.
{3) WhereВ·an employer has established a pension scheme which
is approved by the Minister, the retirement benefits shall be paid in
accordance with such pension scheme.
(4) An employee who retires in agreement with the employer
before attaining the age of fifty-five years shall be paid retirement
benefits in accordance with sub-paragraph (2 ).

sections.heading: An employee whose employment is tenninated on medical Early medical
sections.text:
grounds as certified bya_registered medical practitioner shall be entitled discharge
to tenninal benefits of not less than two and half months pay for each
completed year ofs ervice.
12 . Where an employee spends a night away from home to attend Subsistance
to the business of the employer, they shall be paid an allowance of allowance
fifty thousand kwacha per hight to cover all expenses.

sections.heading: A machine allowance to tailors who provide their own machine Machine
sections.text:
shall be paid at the following rates; allowance
(a) for a hand machine, twenty thousand kwacha per month;
and
(b) for a treadle or electric machine, twenty thousan<;l kwacha
per month.

Lunch 15. An employee shall be entitled to a launch allowance oft wenty
allowance
thousand lcwacha per month unless the employer provides a canteen at
which the employee may obtain wholesome and adequate meals,
provided free of charge.
Rcpartriation 16. An employee, together with his family, shall be transported by
the employer to the employee's place of recruitment or be paid a
repatriation allowance by the employer equal to the current cost of
travelling by public transpo1 t, and tlie most direct route to the employee's
place ofrecruitmcnt, if the employee-
(a) is discharged on medical grounds:
{b) is declared redundant;
(c) retires; or
(d } dies in service, in which case the benefits shall accrue to th_ e
family of the deceased employee.
